Page.Width: {Binding RelativeSource={RelativeSource Mode=FindAncestor, AncestorType={x:Type ContentControl}}, Path=ActualWidth, FallbackValue=700}
Page.Height: {Binding RelativeSource={RelativeSource Mode=FindAncestor, AncestorType={x:Type ContentControl}}, Path=ActualHeight, FallbackValue=700}

TextBlock.Text: Using Maritime Patrol Aircraft for Reconnaissance

Run.Text: By zooming out on the Tactical Display, you can see that the area we are tasked to search is very large (over 500 nautical miles!) Such a large area cannot be searched only by surface ships alone. We need to make use of the air assets at our disposal to begin setting up a search.
Let's start by giving the Tu-95RT a search pattern. First, make sure the aircraft is at cruising altitude and has its radar turned on using the EMCON menu.
Once the Tu-95RT has its radar enabled, go ahead and give it a series of waypoints to move east and west across the marked search area. Remember that you can hold Shift to issue multiple waypoints.
